channel.title: Github on Sujith Quintelier
channel.description: Recent content in Github on Sujith Quintelier

item.title: github: Copilot code review: New configurations and controls
item.description:
GitHub added new ways to configure Copilot code review, including organization runner controls, support for Copilot content exclusion, and removal of the repository custom instructions character limit.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Actions: Minimum version enforcement timeline for self-hosted runners
item.description:
GitHub Actions is resuming enforcement of minimum version requirements for self-hosted runners on github.com and GitHub Enterprise Cloud with Data Residency. The post says this is part of a broader effort to rebuild enforcement, but the excerpt does not include the specific minimum versions or timeline details.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Enterprise Server 3.21 is now generally available
item.description:
GitHub Enterprise Server 3.21 is now generally available. The release adds improvements in deployment efficiency, monitoring, code security, and policy management, including organization custom properties now generally available.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Bot-created pull requests can run workflows if approved
item.description:
Pull requests created by github-actions[bot] can now run CI/CD workflows after a user approves them. The approval step is required as a security check before generated code is allowed to trigger workflows.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: AI usage report updates
item.description:
AI usage reports now include GitHub AI Credits usage in the standard report fields. To track AI credit consumption, use the quantity field for AI credit quantity and gross_amount for the associated amount.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot CLI: Configure everything from one place with /settings
item.description:
GitHub Copilot CLI adds a unified /settings command for managing configuration in one place. It consolidates older commands like /theme, /streamer-mode, and /experimental, along with other previously separate options.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: New runner images in public preview
item.description:
GitHub has added two new GitHub-hosted runner images for Actions in public preview. They are available to all users so workflows can be tested on the latest platforms before general release.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Agentic Workflows is now in public preview
item.description:
GitHub Agentic Workflows is now in public preview. It lets you automate reasoning-based tasks such as issue triage, CI failure analysis, and documentation updates using coding agents inside GitHub.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Agentic workflows no longer need a personal access token
item.description:
GitHub Agentic Workflows can now use the built-in GitHub Actions GITHUB_TOKEN instead of requiring a personal access token. This removes the need to create and store a PAT for these workflows.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: List, view, and create discussions in GitHub CLI
item.description:
GitHub CLI now includes a new gh discussion command group for GitHub Discussions. It lets you list, view, create, and update discussions from the CLI.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Manage sub-issues, types, and dependencies from GitHub CLI
item.description:
GitHub CLI now supports managing issue types, parent/sub-issue relationships, and issue dependencies directly from the terminal. This lets you structure and track issues without using the browser.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot Chat now sees your agent sessions
item.description:
GitHub Copilot Chat on the web now has a better handoff to Copilot cloud agent sessions. It also adds the ability to search and query past agent sessions.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Enterprises can now create up to 500 cost centers
item.description:
GitHub has doubled the enterprise cost center limit from 250 to 500. This applies to enterprises managing many business units, departments, or products.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Incremental analysis for Go, C/C++, and CodeQL CLI
item.description:
CodeQL pull request scans for C/C++ and Go now use incremental analysis, which makes them faster. GitHub says this builds on earlier incremental analysis improvements for other languages and CodeQL CLI.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Dedicated security review command now available in Copilot CLI
item.description:
GitHub Copilot CLI now includes an experimental /security-review slash command for running security reviews on code changes directly in the CLI. The feature is in public preview.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Dependabot version updates now support the Deno ecosystem
item.description:
Dependabot version updates now support Deno. The support is for version updates only, not security updates.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Upcoming breaking changes for npm v12
item.description:
npm v12 will change the default behavior of npm install with security-related defaults. The post says these changes are already available as warnings in npm 11.16.0 and newer.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Claude Fable 5 is generally available for GitHub Copilot
item.description:
Claude Fable 5 from Anthropic is now generally available in GitHub Copilot. GitHub describes it as a Mythos-class model aimed at long-horizon, autonomous coding and knowledge-work tasks.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Periodic code scanning of inactive repositories
item.description:
GitHub code scanning now supports scheduled security scans for repositories with no pushes or pull requests for six months or longer. This helps organizations keep inactive repositories under periodic review.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GPT-4.1 deprecated
item.description:
GitHub deprecated GPT-4.1 in all GitHub Copilot experiences on June 1, 2026. The affected surface areas include Copilot Chat, inline edits, ask and agent modes, and code completions.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Expanded technical preview availability for the GitHub Copilot app
item.description:
GitHub has expanded the technical preview of the Copilot app to all existing Copilot Pro, Pro+, Business, and Enterprise customers. The app is available for download on Windows, macOS, and Linux.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot SDK is now generally available
item.description:
GitHub Copilot SDK is now generally available. It provides a stable API and production-ready support for embedding Copilot’s agentic engine into applications, services, and developer tools.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot CLI: Improved UI, rubber duck, prompt scheduling, and voice input
item.description:
GitHub Copilot CLI is getting a UI refresh, with a new experimental terminal interface and support for tabs. Rubber duck, prompt scheduling, and voice input are generally available.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Cloud and local sandboxes for GitHub Copilot now in public preview
item.description:
GitHub Copilot now supports secure, isolated sandboxes for tool execution in both local and cloud environments, available in public preview. The update is intended to isolate Copilot actions from the rest of the system.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot code review for Azure Repos is now in technical preview
item.description:
GitHub Copilot code review for Azure Repos is in technical preview, adding on-demand pull request reviews inside Azure DevOps. It must be enabled before use.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Shape Copilot code review around your team
item.description:
GitHub is previewing updates to Copilot code review that let it adapt to a team’s tools and standards, and vary review depth based on change complexity. The post says two public previews are shipping on a unified agent.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Extend GitHub with agent apps
item.description:
GitHub now supports “agent apps,” which are AI agents from GitHub partners that can be installed from the GitHub Marketplace and used directly in GitHub. The changelog note says they install like other GitHub Apps.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Introducing Copilot CLI and agentic capabilities enhancements in JetBrains IDEs
item.description:
GitHub added Copilot CLI support to Copilot for JetBrains IDEs and expanded agentic capabilities in the IDE integration. The update focuses on new Copilot CLI session features plus additional workflow improvements.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Gemini models in Copilot CLI, cloud agent, and the Copilot app
item.description:
GitHub added Gemini 3.1 Pro (Preview) and Gemini 3.5 Flash to more Copilot surfaces. The models are now available in Copilot CLI, Copilot cloud agent, and the GitHub Copilot app.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot in Eclipse: BYOK, skills, and chat updates
item.description:
GitHub Copilot in Eclipse adds a refreshed chat experience, more session context visibility, BYOK for Business and Enterprise, improved ABAP support, and skills-related updates. The changelog notes multiple chat and workflow changes, but the provided excerpt does not include full technical details.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Evaluation models in auto for individual plans
item.description:
GitHub Copilot now includes evaluation models for individual non-enterprise users, and these models may be selected by Copilot auto model selection. Users can disable evaluation-model usage in Copilot.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Updates to GitHub Copilot billing and plans
item.description:
GitHub Copilot now uses usage-based billing for all users. Copilot code review also consumes GitHub Actions minutes, in addition to GitHub billing.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot usage metrics API adds cohorts for AI adoption
item.description:
GitHub’s Copilot usage metrics API now classifies engaged users into AI adoption cohorts, giving teams a more detailed view of how Copilot is being used. The change is intended to help measure adoption beyond simple activity counts.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Hard budget limits now available for GitHub Advanced Security
item.description:
GitHub Advanced Security now supports hard budget limits for GHAS SKUs, allowing enterprise admins and billing managers to prevent teams from exceeding allocated license budgets. This applies to license-based GHAS products.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: CodeQL 2.25.5 improves query accuracy for GitHub Actions
item.description:
CodeQL 2.25.5 was released with query accuracy improvements for GitHub Actions, along with additional accuracy improvements across C/C++.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Claude Opus 4.8 is generally available for GitHub Copilot
item.description:
Claude Opus 4.8 is now generally available in GitHub Copilot. GitHub says early testing showed improved code understanding and generation compared with earlier models.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot Memory has more controls for deletion, scope, and the Copilot CLI
item.description:
GitHub updated Copilot Memory with better deletion controls, a repository-level disable option, and additional memory controls in Copilot CLI. The feature remains in public preview.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Code Quality: Repository Enablement API
item.description:
GitHub Code Quality now has a Repository Enablement API in public preview. It adds two new endpoints to programmatically enable and configure Code Quality on individual repositories.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Target Copilot models to organizations with model rules
item.description:
GitHub added targeted Copilot model rules for enterprise owners. This lets them control which Copilot models are available to each organization, instead of applying the same model availability across the whole enterprise.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Dependabot version updates now support the sbt ecosystem
item.description:
Dependabot version updates now support the sbt ecosystem. You can add sbt to dependabot.yml, and Dependabot will monitor build.sbt inputs and open pull requests when newer commits are available.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Filter secret scanning approval requests by sort order and bypass status
item.description:
GitHub is adding two delegated workflow improvements for secret scanning: you can now sort bypass and dismissal requests in the UI and filter approval requests by bypass status. The update is part of the secret scanning approval request workflow.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Classroom sign-ups are no longer available
item.description:
GitHub Classroom no longer accepts new sign-ups as of today. Existing accounts and classrooms can continue to be used while GitHub transitions to partner solutions.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Code coverage on pull requests is now in public preview
item.description:
Code coverage metrics are now in public preview for GitHub Code Quality users on github.com. Pull requests now show an aggregate code coverage percentage directly in the PR view.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Staged publishing and new install-time controls for npm
item.description:
GitHub announced two npm supply-chain security updates: staged publishing is now generally available, and npm install gets new source अनुमति flags for file, remote, and directory dependencies. These new flags extend the existing –allow-git control.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot for Eclipse is open source
item.description:
GitHub Copilot for Eclipse is now open source. The code is available on GitHub under the MIT license.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Issue fields are now in public preview for all organizations
item.description:
GitHub has made issue fields available in public preview for all organizations on github.com and GitHub Enterprise Cloud with data residency. The feature lets teams define typed metadata on issues, such as Priority and Effort.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot usage metrics reports now use GitHub-owned download URLs
item.description:
Copilot usage metrics report downloads now use a GitHub-owned custom domain instead of Azure Front Door URLs. GitHub says this improves URL stability.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Updates to available models in Copilot on web
item.description:
GitHub updated the model selection available for Copilot Chat on the web to improve consistency and response quality. The post says model choice is being limited, but the provided excerpt does not include the full list of changes.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Auto model selection now routes based on your task in VS Code
item.description:
GitHub Copilot auto model selection in VS Code now routes tasks to the best model based on utilization and model health metrics. GitHub says this is intended to improve quality, reliability, and token efficiency.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Semantic issue search in Copilot Chat
item.description:
GitHub Copilot Chat on the web now supports natural-language issue search, grouping, and analysis using a new semantic issues index. Results are context-aware and intended to help users find relevant issues more quickly.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Easily apply Copilot code review feedback with Copilot cloud agent
item.description:
GitHub renamed Copilot code review’s “Implement suggestion” button to “Fix with Copilot”. The updated flow now includes a UI dialog for more control when applying suggestions with the Copilot cloud agent.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Upcoming deprecation of Python 3.9 for Dependabot
item.description:
Dependabot will stop supporting Python 3.9 on June 23, 2026 because Python 3.9 has reached end of life. Users still on 3.9 may see Dependabot issues after the cutoff.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Gemini 3.5 Flash is generally available for GitHub Copilot
item.description:
Gemini 3.5 Flash is now generally available in GitHub Copilot. GitHub says it is rolling out broadly and positions it as a Flash-tier model with near-Pro coding quality, but the note does not include technical details beyond that.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Expanded OIDC support for Dependabot and code scanning
item.description:
Dependabot and code scanning now support OpenID Connect (OIDC) authentication for organization-level private registries on Cloudsmith and Google Artifact Registry.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Start a GitHub Advanced Security trial from a risk assessment
item.description:
Eligible enterprise admins can start a GitHub Advanced Security trial directly from a Secret Protection or Code Security risk assessment. This lets them begin evaluating the products without leaving the assessment flow.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Removal of code_scanning_upload field from rate_limit API endpoint
item.description:
GitHub removed the code_scanning_upload field from the REST API rate_limit endpoint response as of May 19, 2026. The field no longer appears in the resources object.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: One-click fixes for failing Actions with Copilot cloud agent
item.description:
GitHub Actions failures can now be handed to Copilot cloud agent with one click for Copilot Business and Copilot Enterprise subscribers. The new “Fix with Copilot” button starts an automated fix flow for failed jobs.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot cloud agent: Fast, cost-efficient models for simple tasks
item.description:
GitHub Copilot cloud agent now supports additional models, letting users choose fast, cost-efficient options for simpler tasks. The change expands the set of models available when delegating work to the cloud agent.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Ask questions in context with Copilot on web
item.description:
Copilot chat on the web now opens directly on the page you’re viewing, so you can ask questions in context with less switching between pages.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Audit repository Copilot cloud agent configuration via the REST API
item.description:
GitHub added a public preview REST API to get a repository’s Copilot cloud agent configuration. This lets you audit that configuration programmatically.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot Spaces API now generally available
item.description:
GitHub Copilot Spaces API is now generally available. It supports programmatic create, read, update, and delete operations for Spaces from external applications.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Remote control for Copilot CLI sessions now generally available on mobile, web, and VS Code
item.description:
Remote control for Copilot CLI sessions is now generally available on GitHub Mobile and github.com, with support also referenced for VS Code. It lets you start work in the terminal and continue managing the session from mobile or web.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GPT-5.3-Codex is now the base model for Copilot Business and Enterprise
item.description:
GPT-5.3-Codex is now the default base model for all Copilot Business and Copilot Enterprise organizations, replacing GPT-4.1. GitHub says the base model is used when an organization has not selected another model.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub App installation tokens: Per-request override header
item.description:
GitHub is rolling out a new GitHub App installation token format and is providing a per-request override header so developers can validate apps and workflows before the rollout completes.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Grok Code Fast 1 deprecated
item.description:
GitHub deprecated Grok Code Fast 1 across all GitHub Copilot experiences on May 15, 2026. The deprecation applies to Copilot Chat, inline edits, ask and agent modes, and code completions.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot Memory supports user preferences for Pro, Pro+ users
item.description:
Copilot Memory now supports user-level preferences in early access for Copilot Pro and Pro+ users. Copilot can store stated or inferred personal preferences about how you like to work.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Timestamp fields in GitHub Projects
item.description:
GitHub Projects now has three built-in timestamp fields: Created, Updated, and Closed. These fields can be added to any project view for sorting and filtering work by when items were created, last updated, or closed.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Team-level Copilot usage metrics now available via API
item.description:
GitHub Copilot usage metrics API now includes a new user-teams report that maps each Copilot-licensed user to the teams they belong to. This can be joined with existing usage reports to analyze Copilot usage at the team level.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Actions: Upcoming image migrations
item.description:
GitHub Actions has announced two upcoming hosted runner image migrations. GitHub is also transitioning to own and maintain the Arm64 images for hosted runners.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot app is now available in technical preview
item.description:
GitHub Copilot app is available in technical preview as a GitHub-native desktop experience for starting agentic development from existing work while keeping it isolated and steerable.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot cloud agent supports auto model selection
item.description:
Copilot cloud agent now supports Copilot auto model selection. Choosing Auto in the model picker lets Copilot pick the best available model based on system health and model availability.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Introducing Copilot CLI agent and unified sessions view in GitHub Copilot for JetBrains IDEs
item.description:
GitHub Copilot for JetBrains IDEs now includes the Copilot CLI agent and a unified sessions view. The sessions view shows live status for running and queued sessions, and the update also adds an ask… feature.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: New enterprise installation API now in public preview
item.description:
GitHub has introduced a new Enterprise Installation API in public preview for GitHub App developers. The API fills a gap identified from community feedback and lets an app determine whether it is installed in an enterprise.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Start Copilot cloud agent tasks via the REST API
item.description:
Copilot Business and Copilot Enterprise users can now start Copilot cloud agent tasks programmatically using a new Agent tasks REST API. The feature is in public preview.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Enterprise Server 3.21 release candidate is available
item.description:
GitHub Enterprise Server 3.21 release candidate is available. The release focuses on deployment efficiency, monitoring, code security, and policy management, with organization custom properties now generally available.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot code review: Comment experience improvements
item.description:
GitHub updated Copilot code review comments in the new pull requests experience to make them easier to scan and act on. The changes are available to all users opted into that experience and include grouped suggestions, severity levels, and an updated comment layout.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: April reports are now available to prepare for usage-based billing
item.description:
GitHub now lets customers download April usage reports to see how Copilot activity maps to AI credits ahead of the new usage-based billing rollout. The billing unit of AI credits is going live on a staged schedule.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: CodeQL 2.25.4 adds Swift 6.3.1 support, improvements to C# and Java, and more
item.description:
CodeQL 2.25.4 adds support for Swift 6.3.1 and includes improvements for C# and Java. The release also contains additional updates not detailed in the provided excerpt.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Synchronous SBOM API deprecated
item.description:
GitHub has deprecated the older synchronous SBOM REST API after releasing a new asynchronous SBOM API. The synchronous API is scheduled for removal on November 13, 2026, giving a six-month migration window.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Cross-org Dependabot access for internal repositories
item.description:
Dependabot can now access internal repositories in other organizations within the same enterprise. This lets Dependabot resolve dependencies hosted in cross-org internal GitHub repositories.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Create repositories on the go with GitHub Mobile
item.description:
GitHub Mobile now supports creating new repositories directly in the app. On iOS, you can start from Home or Profile, tap the + button, and select Create repository.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Upcoming deprecation of Grok Code Fast 1
item.description:
GitHub will deprecate Grok Code Fast 1 across all GitHub Copilot experiences on May 15, 2026. The deprecation applies to Copilot Chat, inline edits, ask and agent modes, and code completions.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot code review comment types now in usage metrics API
item.description:
The Copilot usage metrics API now reports Copilot code review suggestions broken down by comment type. This adds a new copilot_suggestions_by_comment_type metric for analyzing code review activity.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Disable commit comments on the user level
item.description:
GitHub now lets personal account owners set commit comments to be enabled or disabled by default for their repositories. This adds a user-level default for commit comment behavior across repos they own.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: More flexible secrets and variables for Copilot cloud agent
item.description:
GitHub says Copilot cloud agent now supports more flexible handling of secrets and variables when you delegate tasks to it. The agent runs in a GitHub Actions-backed development environment, and users can pass secrets and variables into that environment.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: CodeQL 2.25.3 adds Swift 6.3 support
item.description:
CodeQL 2.25.3 has been released with support for Swift 6.3. The note does not list any other changes.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Actions concurrency groups now allow larger queues
item.description:
You can now allow multiple jobs or workflow runs to wait in the same GitHub Actions concurrency group instead of being limited to a single pending run. Previously, a concurrency…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Upcoming deprecation of GPT-4.1
item.description:
GitHub will deprecate GPT-4.1 across all Copilot experiences on 2026-06-01. Users are directed to switch to the suggested alternative model.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Claude Sonnet 4 deprecated
item.description:
GitHub deprecated Claude Sonnet 4 across all Copilot experiences on May 6, 2026. The deprecation applies to Copilot Chat, inline edits, ask and agent modes, and code completions.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Enterprise Live Migrations is now in public preview
item.description:
Enterprise Live Migrations (ELM) is now in public preview. It lets enterprise administrators migrate repositories from GitHub Enterprise Server (GHES) to GitHub Enterprise Cloud.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Rubber Duck in GitHub Copilot CLI now supports more models
item.description:
Rubber Duck in GitHub Copilot CLI now supports additional model combinations. It can use a Claude-powered critic agent in GPT sessions, with support also extended for Claude-based sessions.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Repository rulesets: User bypass and branch renaming
item.description:
GitHub repository rulesets now allow individual users to be added as bypass actors. Organization rulesets also now support renaming branches that they cover.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Enterprise-managed plugins in GitHub Copilot CLI are now in public preview
item.description:
GitHub Copilot CLI now supports enterprise-managed plugins in public preview. Enterprise admins can configure and distribute plugins to Copilot CLI users across the organization.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot in Visual Studio Code, April releases
item.description:
GitHub Copilot in Visual Studio Code changelog covers VS Code releases v1.116 through v1.119 from April and early May 2026. The post notes that Copilot can now search by meaning.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Search and filter bar for repository security advisories
item.description:
GitHub added a search bar and filters to the Security tab’s repository security advisories list. This lets you search and narrow advisories directly within a repository.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Secret scanning with GitHub MCP Server is now generally available
item.description:
GitHub secret scanning in the GitHub MCP server is now generally available. It can be used with MCP-compatible AI coding agents or IDEs, including GitHub Copilot CLI.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Dependency scanning with GitHub MCP Server is in public preview
item.description:
GitHub MCP Server now supports dependency scanning in public preview. It can scan code changes for vulnerable dependencies before you commit or open a pull request.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Code-to-cloud risk visibility with Microsoft Defender for Cloud is now generally available
item.description:
GitHub says the code-to-cloud risk visibility integration with Microsoft Defender for Cloud is now generally available. The release includes follow-up improvements since public preview, bringing artifact and runtime context closer together.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Deprecation notice: code_scanning_upload field will be removed from rate_limit API endpoint
item.description:
GitHub will remove the code_scanning_upload field from the rate_limit REST API endpoint response on May 19, 2026. The notice says the field is being deprecated and will no longer be returned after that date.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Upcoming deprecation of GPT-5.2 and GPT-5.2-Codex
item.description:
GitHub says it will deprecate GPT-5.2 and GPT-5.2-Codex across GitHub Copilot experiences, including Chat, inline edits, ask and agent modes, and code completions. The notice says GPT-5.2-Codex is excepted in Copilot, but no replacement or timeline details are included in the provided text.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot in Visual Studio — April update
item.description:
The April 2026 GitHub Copilot update for Visual Studio focuses on agentic workflows. It adds direct cloud agent sessions from the IDE, user-level support for custom agents, and a new Debugger agent for validation.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot Student GPT-5.3-Codex removal from model picker
item.description:
GitHub is removing GPT-5.3-Codex from the Copilot Student plan’s model picker starting today. The model is still available when using auto model selection.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot cloud agent starts 20% faster with Actions custom images
item.description:
Copilot cloud agent startup is now over 20% faster. The improvement comes from optimized runner environments built with GitHub Actions custom images.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot code review will start consuming GitHub Actions minutes on June 1, 2026
item.description:
GitHub Copilot code review will begin consuming GitHub Actions minutes starting June 1, 2026. The change applies to code review usage on pull requests.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Notice about upcoming new format for GitHub App installation tokens
item.description:
GitHub will begin a staged rollout on April 27, 2026 to change the format of newly minted GitHub App installation tokens. The update is intended to improve token performance and will happen over several weeks.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Changes to notification retention and archived repository watches
item.description:
GitHub is changing how long notifications are retained and how watches on archived repositories are handled. The rollout is expected to finish over the next few months.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GPT-5.5 is generally available for GitHub Copilot
item.description:
GPT-5.5 is now generally available in GitHub Copilot. GitHub says early testing showed the model performs best on complex, multi-step agentic coding tasks and real-world problem solving.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Inline agent mode in preview and more in GitHub Copilot for JetBrains IDEs
item.description:
GitHub Copilot for JetBrains IDEs adds inline agent mode in preview, along with improvements to Next Edit Suggestions and new controls for approving terminal commands and file edits. The update also introduces global auto-approve and other related enhancements.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot Chat improvements for pull requests
item.description:
GitHub Copilot Chat has added richer context and new capabilities for working with diffs and pull requests. The feature is available when you ask questions about a pull request in Copilot Chat.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Global pull requests dashboard moves to opt-out public preview
item.description:
GitHub is moving the new global pull requests dashboard to an opt-out public preview, so it will be enabled by default for all users. The new dashboard and inbox experience is transitioning from opt-in to opt-out.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot cloud agent fields added to usage metrics
item.description:
GitHub added a new used_copilot_cloud_agent boolean field to the Copilot usage metrics API in user-level reports. It mirrors the existing Copilot coding agent field after the rename to Copilot cloud agent.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Dependabot-based dependency graphs for Python
item.description:
GitHub now uses Dependabot-based data to generate more complete and accurate transitive dependency trees for Python dependency graphs and SBOMs. This change improves coverage for Python projects.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Disable commit comments across your organization
item.description:
GitHub now lets organization owners disable commit comments at the org level, removing the need to change each repository individually. This follows a prior repo-level option to disable commit comments.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Better debugging with GitHub Copilot on the web
item.description:
GitHub Copilot Chat on github.com now better handles pasted stack traces, helping users identify the root cause of errors faster. The update improves stack trace recognition reliability in the web experience.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: View and manage agent sessions from issues and projects
item.description:
GitHub now lets you view and steer cloud agent sessions directly from issues and projects. This adds visibility into agent activity without leaving the current workflow.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Pausing new self-serve signups for GitHub Copilot Business
item.description:
GitHub is pausing new self-serve signups for GitHub Copilot Business for some organizations. The change is described as part of ongoing efforts to keep the Copilot experience reliable and sustainable.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot for Jira: Our latest enhancements
item.description:
GitHub says it has added new enhancements to the GitHub Copilot cloud agent for Jira integration. The update focuses on making the integration more powerful and customizable, with more control for teams.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Upcoming change to Copilot usage metrics report download URLs
item.description:
GitHub is changing Copilot usage metrics report download links from Azure Front Door domains to a stable GitHub-owned custom domain. The update is intended to improve URL stability.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot code review user counts now aggregate in usage metrics API
item.description:
The Copilot usage metrics API now aggregates Copilot code review active and passive user counts in enterprise and organization usage reports. This follows the launch of active and passive user identification for Copilot code review.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub CLI: Opt-out usage telemetry
item.description:
GitHub CLI v2.91.0 adds pseudonymous usage telemetry by default. The release notes say the data is used to improve the product and is opt-out.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Bring your own language model key in VS Code now available
item.description:
Copilot Business and Enterprise users can now use bring your own language model key (BYOK) in Visual Studio Code. This lets teams use their own API keys to access supported models in VS Code.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: C++ code intelligence for GitHub Copilot CLI in public preview
item.description:
The Microsoft C++ Language Server is now in public preview for GitHub Copilot CLI. It uses the IntelliSense engine from Visual Studio and VS Code to extend C++ code intelligence in the CLI.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Deprecation of security-related organization API fields
item.description:
We have deprecated the following fields from the get an organization and update an organization REST API endpoints: advanced_security_enabled_for_new_repositories dependabot_alerts_enabled_for_new_repositories dependabot_security_updates_enabled_for_new_repositories dependency_graph_enabled_for_new_repositories secret_scanning_enabled_for_new_repositories secret_scanning_push_protection_enabled_for_new_repositories secret_scanning_validity_checks_enabled GitHub Code Security configurations now…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: CodeQL now supports sanitizers and validators in models-as-data
item.description:
CodeQL data extensions now support defining custom sanitizers and validators in models-as-data. This adds new ways to model how data is cleaned or checked in code scanning rules.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Changes to GitHub Copilot plans for individuals
item.description:
GitHub is changing Copilot plans for individual users. The post says the changes are intended to support service reliability and sustainability, but the provided excerpt does not include the specific plan details.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Sunsetting SHA-1 in HTTPS on GitHub
item.description:
GitHub says it will remove SHA-1 use in HTTPS for GitHub and its CDNs. This change affects browsers and other software that connect to or view GitHub over HTTPS.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot CLI now supports Copilot auto model selection
item.description:
GitHub Copilot CLI now generally supports Copilot auto model selection for all Copilot plans. When enabled, Copilot picks the most efficient model automatically.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot CLI supports custom registry based MCP allowlists
item.description:
Enterprise and organization administrators can now bring their own MCP registry and enforce allowlist policies in Copilot CLI. This feature is in public preview and brings MCP governance controls to…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Manage agent skills with GitHub CLI
item.description:
GitHub CLI now includes gh skill, a new command for discovering and installing agent skills. The post says it is intended to make managing skills easier for AI coding agents.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Rule insights dashboard and unified filter bar
item.description:
GitHub announced a new rule insights dashboard and a unified filter bar for repository rulesets. The update is meant to make it easier to spot trends such as blocked-push spikes and bypass patterns.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Claude Opus 4.7 is generally available
item.description:
Claude Opus 4.7 is now generally available in GitHub Copilot. GitHub says early testing showed improved multi-step task performance and more reliable agentic execution.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: CodeQL 2.25.2 adds Kotlin 2.3.20 support and other updates
item.description:
CodeQL 2.25.2 has been released. It adds support for Kotlin 2.3.20 and includes other unspecified updates.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Enable Copilot cloud agent via custom properties
item.description:
GitHub Copilot cloud agent can now be enabled selectively for specific organizations using custom properties. Before this change, enterprise admins and AI managers could only turn the agent on or off across all organizations.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: OIDC support for Dependabot and code scanning
item.description:
Dependabot and code scanning now support OpenID Connect (OIDC) authentication for private registries configured at the organization level. This removes the need to store long-lived credentials as repository secrets.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Deployment context in repository properties and alerts
item.description:
GitHub added deployment context to repository properties and security alert pages. Two new built-in repository properties, deployable and deployed, now expose this information.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Link code scanning alerts to GitHub Issues
item.description:
GitHub added the ability to link code scanning alerts to GitHub Issues. The feature is in public preview and is meant to connect security remediation work with existing planning and tracking workflows.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Dependabot and code scanning: Org-level private registries
item.description:
GitHub now lets organizations configure multiple private registries at the org level for Dependabot and code scanning, which is useful for setups that use more than one internal package feed. Previously, org-level settings supported only one private registry per ecosystem.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Secret scanning pattern updates and product improvements
item.description:
GitHub says it is rolling out secret scanning updates that improve detection coverage, APIs, and related workflows. The post says these changes are part of ongoing secret scanning product improvements, but the provided excerpt does not include具体 details.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: SBOM exports are now computed asynchronously
item.description:
SBOM exports from repository dependency graph pages and the new API endpoints are now computed asynchronously. This changes export handling from a synchronous action to an async one.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Model selection for Claude and Codex agents on github.com
item.description:
GitHub.com now lets users select the model when starting Claude and Codex third-party coding agent runs. The feature is available for these agents, similar to Copilot cloud agent.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Code Quality: Improvements to standard findings in public preview
item.description:
GitHub Code Quality has updated its standard findings experience in public preview to make repository-wide navigation and triage easier. The changelog notes improvements focused on finding reliability and related issues, but does not provide detailed feature specifics in the excerpt.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot data residency in US + EU and FedRAMP compliance now available
item.description:
GitHub Copilot now supports data residency in US and EU regions, keeping inference processing and related data within the selected geography. It also adds FedRAMP compliance for US government customers.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Fix merge conflicts in three clicks with Copilot cloud agent
item.description:
GitHub added a new “Fix with Copilot” button on github.com for resolving merge conflicts, powered by Copilot cloud agent. The feature lets users fix conflicts from a comment flow in three clicks.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Remote control CLI sessions on web and mobile in public preview
item.description:
GitHub Copilot CLI now supports remote control via copilot --remote, allowing users to monitor and steer a running CLI session from web and mobile. The feature is available in public preview.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Enforcing new limits and retiring Opus 4.6 Fast from Copilot Pro+
item.description:
GitHub is enforcing new usage limits for Copilot Pro+ and retiring Opus 4.6 Fast from that plan. The change is intended to address high-concurrency and heavy-usage patterns.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot usage metrics now aggregate Copilot cloud agent active user counts
item.description:
GitHub is renaming Copilot coding agent to Copilot cloud agent and updating the data schema to reflect that change. Copilot usage metrics will now aggregate active user counts for the cloud agent under the new naming.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Pausing new GitHub Copilot Pro trials
item.description:
GitHub is pausing new GitHub Copilot Pro free trials because of increased abuse of the trial system. The change is intended to protect platform integrity for legitimate developers.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Actions workflows are limited to 50 reruns
item.description:
GitHub Actions workflow runs are now limited to 50 reruns per workflow. Attempts to rerun a workflow beyond that limit will fail with a failed check suite.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot CLI activity now included in usage metrics totals and feature breakdowns
item.description:
GitHub Copilot usage metrics now include CLI activity in the overall totals and feature breakdowns. Previously, CLI metrics were only available separately in a standalone totals_by_cli section.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot cloud agent’s validation tools are now 20% faster
item.description:
GitHub says the validation tools used by Copilot cloud agent are now 20% faster. The tools include CodeQL, the GitHub Advisory Database, secret scanning, and Copilot code review.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Ask Copilot in security assessments now available
item.description:
GitHub added an Ask Copilot entry point in security assessment results, letting organization admins and security managers get contextual explanations and next-step guidance directly from secret risk assessment and Code Security risk assessment views.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: New Low Quality option in the Hide comment menu
item.description:
GitHub moderators can now mark hidden comments as Low Quality from the Hide comment menu. The option is available on issues, discussions, pull requests, and commits.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: New Sort by control added to Notifications
item.description:
GitHub Notifications now includes a new “Sort by” control with two options: “Newest to oldest” and “Oldest to newest.” The default behavior remains “Newest to oldest.”

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Repository member role labels now in pull request list view
item.description:
GitHub now shows repository member role labels inline in the pull request list view for public repositories. Labels such as First-time contributor, Contributor, and Member appear directly in the list.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Release information in issue sidebar and default values for project fields
item.description:
GitHub added release tracking details to the issue sidebar, configurable default values for project fields, and faster navigation between related issues.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot-reviewed pull request merge metrics now in the usage metrics API
item.description:
The Copilot usage metrics API now includes two new merge metrics for Copilot-reviewed pull requests, extending the pull request throughput and cycle-time metrics added in February. These metrics track PRs reviewed by Copilot from the coding agent.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Secret scanning improvements to alert APIs, webhooks, and delegated workflows
item.description:
GitHub is rolling out updates to secret scanning alert APIs, webhooks, and delegated workflows. The changes are aimed at improving the developer experience for secret scanning features.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Mobile: Research and code with Copilot cloud agent anywhere
item.description:
GitHub Mobile now supports Copilot cloud agent beyond pull request workflows, letting users continue work away from their desk. The update adds the ability to ask Copilot to research and code from mobile.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Code Security risk assessment available for organizations
item.description:
Organization admins and security managers can now run a free Code Security risk assessment for their organization. It summarizes vulnerabilities by severity, rule type, and related categories.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: New PGP signing key for GitHub CLI Linux packages
item.description:
GitHub CLI’s Linux package repositories now use an updated PGP keyring that includes both the existing signing key and a replacement key. The change is meant to support package signing continuity and will matter if you refresh or verify repository keys.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot in Visual Studio Code, March Releases
item.description:
VS Code moved to weekly stable releases, and this changelog covers Copilot-related updates in VS Code versions 1.111 through 1.115 shipped in March and early April 2026. The post says the highlights include Autopilot for fully automated workflows, but the provided excerpt does not include the detailed release items.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Prioritize security alerts with runtime context from Dynatrace
item.description:
GitHub Advanced Security alerts can now be prioritized using runtime context from Dynatrace, including deployed artifacts and runtime risk in Kubernetes environments. This adds environment-aware signal to help rank alerts.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot CLI now supports BYOK and local models
item.description:
GitHub Copilot CLI now supports connecting your own model provider (BYOK) or using fully local models instead of GitHub-hosted model routing. The change gives CLI users more control over where models run and which providers they use.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Dependabot alerts are now assignable to AI agents for remediation
item.description:
Dependabot alerts can now be assigned to AI coding agents for remediation, including Copilot, Claude, and Codex. This is intended for vulnerabilities that require code changes beyond a simple dependency version update.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Dependabot version updates now support the Nix ecosystem
item.description:
Dependabot version updates now support the Nix ecosystem via Nix flakes. You can add nix as a package ecosystem in dependabot.yml and Dependabot will monitor flake.lock inputs and open pull requests when updates are available.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Code scanning: Batch apply security alert suggestions on pull requests
item.description:
GitHub code scanning alerts on pull requests can now be fixed in bulk from the Files changed tab. The update lets users apply multiple security alert suggestions at once instead of handling them individually.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: npm trusted publishing now supports CircleCI
item.description:
npm trusted publishing now supports CircleCI as an OIDC provider for publishing packages to npm. Maintainers can authenticate from CircleCI workflows without stored credentials, alongside existing support for GitHub Actions and GitLab CI/CD.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot usage metrics now identify active and passive Copilot code review users
item.description:
GitHub Copilot usage metrics now show which users have Copilot code review activity and whether that activity was active or passive. Enterprise and organization admins can use these metrics to see how users engage with Copilot code review.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Organization runner controls for Copilot cloud agent
item.description:
GitHub added organization-level runner controls for Copilot cloud agent development environments. Copilot cloud agent tasks now start on GitHub Actions, and orgs can control which runners are used instead of relying only on the default GitHub-hosted runner.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GPT-5.1 Codex, GPT-5.1-Codex-Max, and GPT-5.1-Codex-Mini deprecated
item.description:
GitHub deprecated GPT-5.1 Codex, GPT-5.1-Codex-Max, and GPT-5.1-Codex-Mini across all GitHub Copilot experiences on April 1, 2026. The change applies to Copilot Chat, inline edits, ask and agent modes, and code completions.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Organization firewall settings for Copilot cloud agent
item.description:
GitHub added organization-level firewall settings for Copilot cloud agent. The built-in agent firewall can now be configured centrally to control internet access and help reduce prompt injection and data exfiltration risk.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot cloud agent signs its commits
item.description:
Copilot cloud agent now signs every commit it creates. These commits show up as Verified on GitHub, indicating they were made by the agent and not altered after signing.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot SDK in public preview
item.description:
GitHub Copilot SDK is now in public preview. It provides building blocks to embed Copilot’s agentic capabilities into applications, workflows, and platform services.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot usage metrics now includes per-user GitHub Copilot CLI activity in organization reports
item.description:
GitHub Copilot organization reports now include per-user GitHub Copilot CLI activity. This completes CLI metrics coverage across enterprise-, user-, and organization-level reporting.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Actions: Early April 2026 updates
item.description:
GitHub Actions added entrypoint and command overrides for service containers. The update also includes new security features: OIDC custom properties and VNET failover.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot in Visual Studio — March update
item.description:
GitHub Copilot in Visual Studio got extensibility updates in the March 2026 release, including custom agents, agent skills, and new tools that improve agent capabilities.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: The Security tab is now Security & quality
item.description:
GitHub has renamed the top-level Security tab to Security & quality across repositories, organizations, and enterprises on github.com. The navigation now groups code quality findings together with security-related information.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Improved search for GitHub Issues is now generally available
item.description:
GitHub says improved search for Issues is now generally available. It was previously in public preview and later added to the Issues dashboard.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot organization custom instructions are generally available
item.description:
GitHub Copilot organization custom instructions are now generally available. Copilot Business and Copilot Enterprise org admins can set default instructions for their organization.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Research, plan, and code with Copilot cloud agent
item.description:
Copilot cloud agent (formerly known as Copilot coding agent) is no longer limited to pull-request workflows, unlocking a broader range of ways to put Copilot to work. More control over…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Codespaces is now generally available for GitHub Enterprise with data residency
item.description:
GitHub Codespaces is now generally available for GitHub Enterprise Cloud with data residency. Enterprises can use cloud development environments with configurable security while keeping data residency requirements in place.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GPT-5.4 mini is now available in Copilot Student auto model selection
item.description:
GPT-5.4 mini is now generally available to Copilot Student users through Copilot auto model selection. It is included in Auto for GitHub Copilot Chat in supported clients such as Visual Studio Code and Visual Studio.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Mobile: Stay in flow with a refreshed Copilot tab and native session logs
item.description:
GitHub Mobile now includes a refreshed Copilot tab, native session logs, and additional in-app controls for agent sessions. The update is aimed at making it easier to manage agentic workflows from the mobile app.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Mobile: Faster, more flexible agent assignment from issues
item.description:
GitHub Mobile adds an Assign an Agent option in the issue overflow menu, making it faster to delegate work from an issue. The update is focused on quicker access to agent assignment and more flexible delegation from mobile.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Upcoming deprecation of Claude Sonnet 4 in GitHub Copilot
item.description:
GitHub will deprecate Claude Sonnet 4 in all GitHub Copilot experiences on 2026-05-01. Users are directed to switch to the suggested alternative model.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Research, plan, and code with Copilot cloud agent
item.description:
GitHub says Copilot cloud agent, formerly Copilot coding agent, is no longer limited to pull-request workflows. This expands how it can be used for research, planning, and coding tasks.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Dependabot now supports Xcode projects using SwiftPM with .xcodeproj manifests
item.description:
Dependabot now supports Swift package dependencies in Xcode projects that use .xcodeproj bundles to manage packages, even when there is no Package.swift file. It can detect and update those dependencies directly from the Xcode project manifest.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub secret scanning — coverage update
item.description:
GitHub secret scanning added nine new secret detectors from seven providers as part of a coverage update. The update includes detectors for providers such as Langchain, Salesforce, and Figma, along with additional secret types from Figma, Google, and OpenVSX.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: CodeQL 2.25.0 adds Swift 6.2.4 support
item.description:
CodeQL 2.25.0 has been released and updates Swift analysis support to version 6.2.4. This release affects GitHub code scanning users who analyze Swift code with CodeQL.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: CodeQL pull requests insights on security overview now cover all protected branches
item.description:
CodeQL pull request insights in GitHub Security Overview now include Copilot Autofix and alert statistics from all protected branches, instead of only the default branch.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: EU data residency region expanding to include EFTA countries
item.description:
Starting May 1, 2026, GitHub Enterprise Cloud on ghe.com will expand the EU data residency region to include Azure infrastructure in EFTA countries, specifically Norway and Switzerland. This affects the EU data residency region for customers using GitHub Enterprise Cloud on ghe.com.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Create issues from Slack with Copilot
item.description:
GitHub now lets you create Issues from Slack using the GitHub app and natural-language prompts. Mention @GitHub in a Slack channel, describe the work, and it will create the issue in GitHub.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Gemini 3 Pro deprecated
item.description:
GitHub has deprecated Gemini 3 Pro across all GitHub Copilot experiences as of March 26, 2026. This includes Copilot Chat, inline edits, ask and agent modes, and code completions.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Credential revocation API now supports GitHub OAuth and GitHub app credentials
item.description:
GitHub extended the Credential revocation API to support GitHub OAuth and GitHub App credentials. The API can now be used to programmatically revoke exposed credentials found in repositories or elsewhere.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Ask @copilot to resolve merge conflicts on pull requests
item.description:
GitHub Copilot coding agent can now resolve merge conflicts on pull requests. Users can trigger it by mentioning @copilot in a PR comment and asking it to handle the conflicts.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: New pull requests dashboard is in public preview
item.description:
GitHub has released a public preview of a refreshed pull requests dashboard at github.com/pulls. It adds a new pull request inbox and saved views for organizing and prioritizing pull requests.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: View Agentic Workflow configs in the Actions run summary
item.description:
GitHub Actions run summaries now show Agentic Workflow markdown configs for agentic workflows. This makes it easier to review runs without leaving the summary page.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Agent activity in GitHub Issues and Projects
item.description:
GitHub added support for surfacing coding agent sessions in Issues and Projects, so agent activity can be tracked directly in planning workflows. The update includes agent sessions shown under assignees in GitHub Issues and agent activity integration in GitHub Projects.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Custom images for GitHub-hosted runners are now generally available
item.description:
Custom images for GitHub-hosted runners are now generally available. The feature, first released in public preview in October 2025, lets users build on GitHub-curated base images for hosted runners.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot for Jira — Public preview enhancements
item.description:
GitHub announced enhancements to the public preview of GitHub Copilot coding agent for Jira based on early customer feedback. The provided excerpt does not include the specific feature changes.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Updates to our Privacy Statement and Terms of Service: How we use your data
item.description:
GitHub updated its Privacy Statement and Terms of Service to clarify how it handles user data. The changes take effect on April 24.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot usage metrics now identify active Copilot coding agent users
item.description:
GitHub Copilot usage metrics now show which users have Copilot coding agent (CCA) activity. Enterprise and organization admins can use this to identify active CCA users by day and other usage views.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Disable comments on individual commits
item.description:
Repository admins can now disable comments on individual commits. GitHub added a new “Commits” section in repository settings to manage this behavior.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Upcoming deprecation of security-related organization API fields
item.description:
On April 21, 2026, we’re deprecating and removing the following fields from the get an organization and update an organization REST API endpoints: advanced_security_enabled_for_new_repositories dependabot_alerts_enabled_for_new_repositories dependabot_security_updates_enabled_for_new_repositories dependency_graph_enabled_for_new_repositories secret_scanning_enabled_for_new_repositories secret_scanning_push_protection_enabled_for_new_repositories secret_scanning_validity_checks_enabled…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Ask @copilot to make changes to any pull request
item.description:
GitHub now lets you mention @copilot in any pull request to request changes directly in the PR. One announced use case is fixing failing GitHub Actions workflows and tests.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Manage Copilot coding agent repository access via the API
item.description:
GitHub introduced public preview REST APIs for managing Copilot coding agent repository access programmatically at the organization level. This lets organization owners control access at scale through the API instead of only manual settings.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Faster incremental analysis with CodeQL in pull requests
item.description:
CodeQL pull request scans for C#, Java, JavaScript/TypeScript, Python, and Ruby are now incremental, which improves scan speed. GitHub had already sped up pull request scans earlier this year.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Gemini 3.1 Pro is now available in JetBrains IDEs, Xcode, and Eclipse
item.description:
Gemini 3.1 Pro is now in public preview for Copilot Enterprise, Business, Pro, and Pro+ users. It can be accessed in GitHub Copilot Chat and in JetBrains IDEs, Xcode, and Eclipse.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Push protection exemptions from repository settings
item.description:
GitHub now lets repository admins manage secret scanning push protection exemptions from a repository’s settings. Before this change, exemptions were only configurable through organization- and enterprise-level security configurations.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot usage metrics now resolve auto model selection to actual models
item.description:
Copilot usage metrics now map activity that was previously shown as “Auto” to the actual model used when auto model selection is enabled. This gives clearer model-level reporting in Copilot usage data.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Trace any Copilot coding agent commit to its session logs
item.description:
GitHub says commits made by Copilot coding agent are authored by Copilot with the assigning human listed as co-author, and that these commits can now be traced back to the agent session logs. This is meant to make it easier to inspect how a given commit was produced.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Monitor Copilot coding agent logs live in Raycast
item.description:
GitHub says the Copilot extension for Raycast can now be used to monitor Copilot coding agent logs live. The post links Raycast’s launcher and extension support with GitHub Copilot agent log viewing.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: A smoother navigation experience in GitHub Mobile for Android
item.description:
GitHub Mobile for Android has updated its navigation to be more consistent and intuitive across the app. The change focuses on smoother movement between key areas like Home and other main sections.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: More visibility into Copilot coding agent sessions
item.description:
GitHub added session log visibility for Copilot coding agent tasks. You can now review what the agent did while it worked in the background before approving its changes.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Actions: Late March 2026 updates
item.description:
GitHub Actions added support for time zones in scheduled workflows and the ability to use environments without automatic deployments. This release focuses on removing a couple of workflow configuration limitations.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Actions Runner Controller release 0.14.0
item.description:
Actions Runner Controller (ARC) 0.14.0 is generally available. The release adds multilabel support for runner scale sets, switches to the actions/scaleset library client, and introduces resource customization options.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot coding agent now starts work 50% faster
item.description:
GitHub says Copilot coding agent now starts work 50% faster. The update applies when handing work to the agent through supported entry points such as assigning an issue to Copilot.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Codespaces with data residency now available in Japan
item.description:
GitHub Codespaces with data residency now supports Japan as a region. This adds Japan to the existing EU and Australia data residency regions.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: View code and comments side-by-side in pull request Files changed page
item.description:
GitHub is rolling out docked panels on pull request “Files changed” pages so reviewers can keep pull request context open side-by-side while reviewing code. The panels include overview, comments, and merge status.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Hierarchy view in GitHub Projects is now generally available
item.description:
Hierarchy view in GitHub Projects is now generally available. It is enabled by default for all new project views, and existing views can be updated to use it.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Configure Copilot coding agent’s validation tools
item.description:
GitHub says Copilot coding agent now automatically runs project tests and linting, plus GitHub validation tools such as CodeQL, advisory database checks, and secret scanning. The new update lets you configure which validation tools the agent uses.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GPT-5.3-Codex long-term support in GitHub Copilot
item.description:
GitHub introduced GPT-5.3-Codex as a long-term support (LTS) model for GitHub Copilot, aimed at giving enterprises a stable option for internal security and safety reviews. The LTS commitment is available for Copilot Business and Copilot plans.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot coding agent works faster with semantic code search
item.description:
Copilot coding agent now supports a semantic code search tool, so it can find relevant code by meaning instead of exact text matches. GitHub says this should make the agent faster when working in codebases.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Dependabot now detects malware in npm dependencies
item.description:
Dependabot can now alert on npm dependencies that match known malware advisories. When malware alerting is enabled, it checks repository npm dependencies against malicious package versions.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Secret scanning in AI coding agents via the GitHub MCP Server
item.description:
GitHub MCP Server can now scan code changes for exposed secrets before commit or pull request creation. The feature is intended to catch credential leaks early in AI coding agent workflows.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Enterprise Server 3.20 is now generally available
item.description:
GitHub Enterprise Server 3.20 is generally available. The release focuses on deployment efficiency, monitoring, code security, and policy management, with an improved merge experience among the highlighted changes.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GPT-5.4 mini is now generally available for GitHub Copilot
item.description:
GPT-5.4 mini is now generally available in GitHub Copilot and is rolling out to users. GitHub says early tests show it as OpenAI’s highest-performing mini agentic coding model.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot usage metrics now includes organization-level GitHub Copilot CLI activity
item.description:
GitHub Copilot usage metrics now includes organization-level GitHub Copilot CLI activity. Organization admins can view CLI-specific activity and usage totals in 1-day usage metrics, completing telemetry coverage across enterprise, user, and organization levels.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Push protection exemptions for roles, teams, and apps
item.description:
GitHub added push protection exemptions for secret scanning at the organization level. Specific roles, teams, and GitHub Apps can now be exempted, with exemption status checked on each push.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Advanced Security setup made simple
item.description:
GitHub Advanced Security now has a guided setup experience for organizations. It lets admins set up and configure Advanced Security more easily, including editing configurations and repository settings.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Code Quality permissions removed from security manager role
item.description:
GitHub changed repository permissions for Code Quality: security managers can no longer enable or disable GitHub Code Quality unless they are also repository administrators. The ability to toggle Code Quality is now limited to repository admins.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Code Quality: Batch apply quality suggestions on pull requests
item.description:
GitHub Code Quality findings on pull requests are now easier to address with bulk actions. You can now apply fixes for Code Quality findings in the Files changed tab by…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Deprecating the cost center integration on the enterprise People page
item.description:
We are deprecating the cost center integration on the enterprise People page and in the People/Licensing CSV export. When the cost center integration was originally shipped in 2023, cost center…

  • Source: GitHub
